Football.title: ‘I can’t get my head around it’ – Michael Carrick ‘baffled’ by Man Utd penalty decision in rare display of emotion
Football.content:
MANCHESTER UNITED coach Michael Carrick fumed at the VAR process in their 2-2 draw with Bournemouth.

United were aggrieved that Amad was denied a penalty by referee Stuart Attwell at 1-0, 25 seconds before Ryan Christie equalised in the 67th minute.





The Premier League Match Centre said it “was deemed the contact was not sufficient for a foul”.

After United made it 2-1, Harry Maguire was sent off for bringing down Evanilson inside the area.

The Match Centre explained that Maguire committed “a holding offence with no attempt to challenge for the ball”.

Junior Kroupi converted from 12 yards in the 81st minute to secure a point for the Cherries against 10-man United.

United broke the deadlock from the penalty spot after Alex Jimenez’s blatant tug on Matheus Cunha allowed Bruno Fernandes to score.

But Carrick said United should have had two penalties.

“My first (thought) is he definitely got one of them wrong, because he’s given one penalty for the same thing that he’s not given as a two-armed grab.

“So the Matheus one, he gives, the second one on Amad he doesn’t. Which is, I think, almost identical, really.



SUN VEGAS WELCOME OFFER: GET £50 BONUS WHEN YOU JOIN

“Two hands on someone in the box, and they go over and they’re in control of the ball.

“For me, it’s two penalties. But it’d be interesting to see which one he acknowledges is wrong, whether the one we got or the one that we didn’t get.

“To give one and not give the other, I can’t get my head around it, I think it’s crazy.

“And it’s a huge moment. You don’t give it, they go down to the other end and score.

“And then it needs to be a bigger penalty to overturn just because they scored when actually it’s a penalty and it should be a penalty if you’ve already given one.



“So a bit baffling, really, to make sense of that. I haven’t spoken to (the ref). Once it’s done, it’s done, you know?

“I think you can have all the conversations in the world about, could it have been this, could it have been that?

“I just think surely that’s for VAR. If they’ve already just given they’ve one and agreed that that was a penalty.



“Surely the next one should be a straightforward one.

“As I say, one of them has got to be right, or they’re wrong or whatever.

“But whether [the officials] apologise or don’t, it’s done now.

United captain Fernandes backed Carrick: “I think we could have gone 2-0 up.

“And then we ended up conceding a goal, not getting a penalty.



“And then we get a penalty against, where more or less it’s the same situation as Amad.

“One is rewarded as a penalty, the other one is not.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

“I know it’s difficult for the referee to give two penalties in the same game for the same team but what I don’t understand is why VAR doesn’t get involved in that situation.

“Either one is a penalty and the other one too or none of them is a penalty.”

Football.title: Weekend betting column: SunSport picks for League Cup final and Premier League crunch games
Football.content:
SunSport’s Kevin Dunne is back with his picks for a huge FA Cup weekend.

ARSENAL haven’t won many League Cups, just the two in fact of the 65 played since it started in 1960-61.

And if you score the winner for the Gunners tomorrow, keep at least one eye open for Tony Adams rushing on the pitch when the whistle goes.



The last time the North London giants won the competition in 1993, matchwinner Steve Morrow was enjoying his moment when skipper Adams picked him up in the celebrations, then dropped him, breaking his arm.

While Mikel Arteta’s side have had no luck in the competition for 33 years now, that is not the case for tomorrow’s opponents in the Carabao Cup final at Wembley, Man City.



They have eight League Cup triumphs on their Roll of Honour, including four-in-a-row from 2018. And that first one was a 3-0 win over the Gunners, courtesy of goals from Sergio Aguero, Vincent Kompany and David Silva.

It’s more than fair to say that Pep Guardiola’s current crop at the Etihad, good though they are, are not quite of the same vintage as that side – as their failure to win a trophy since 2024’s league title illustrates.

So not surprisingly the bookies have chalked up Arsenal as 11-8 favourites to win in 90 minutes tomorrow, a best price with Ladbrokes, Coral and Betfred.

BET BALLS Brighton to beat Liverpool BTTS today is 15-4 with BetGoodwin

Newcastle to beat Sunderland 3-0 tomorrow is 10-1 with BetGoodwin

Spurs to beat Forest 3-1 tomorrow is 14-1 with BetGoodwin

Arsenal to win from behind v Man City tomorrow is 9-1 with BetGoodwin Get a 10% bonus on all winning accas – 18+ T&Cs apply

City aren’t far off though, rated as a best-priced 9-4 shot with bet365, with the draw yours at 12-5 with Betfred.



But Coral’s John Hill doesn’t fancy City to upset the book.

He said: “They’ve run out of steam at the wrong time in the league. And this City team isn’t half as good as the peak teams Guardiola’s had, so I think Arsenal are going to win their first piece of silverware of the season.

“They’re 4-6 to lift the trophy and Man City are 11-10 but I think the Gunners can get the job done in 90 mins at 11-8.

“I also expect there to be plenty of goals, so I‘m backing Arsenal to win and both teams to score.”



That seems a pretty fair shout, at a best-priced 18-5 with Unibet, 888 and William Hill.

While the only piece of real form this season is the 1-1 draw in the league at the Emirates last September, City haven’t beaten the Gunners in any competition since April 2023.

And watching Erling Haaland troop off during Wednesday’s crushing Champions League defeat to Real Madrid, was sadly, not that much of a shock.

By December 20, the City striker had scored 38 goals for club and country. Since then just five. Without him in full flow, City are not the beast they were.

Contrastingly, Arsenal are growing in confidence and banging out the results.



Then there’s the impact of Max Dowman. The 16-year-old’s runaway goal to finish off Everton last weekend was greeted like the arrival of the Messiah at the Emirates.

He’s also a 10-1 shot with BetGoodwin to make England’s World Cup squad this summer. Why not?

Another why not is Arsenal to win the league, Champions League, FA Cup, Carabao Cup Quad – a best-priced 18-1 with MGM.

Especially now Mikel has taken Arte-choke off the menu.

Goodwinners’ Enclosure

WEDNESDAY’S child is full of woe goes the old nursery rhyme – and we certainly were here at BetGoodwin Towers this week!



All four home favourites in the Champions League games – Liverpool, Barcelona, Bayern Munich and Spurs – won, and along with 23 goals going in, meant it wasn’t pretty reading figures wise for us.

Still Man City being held to a 1-1 draw against the Hammers and Spurs nicking a point late on at Anfield in the Prem did save us a right few quid last weekend.

Talking of Spurs, we’ve eased them out to 2-1 in the relegation market, with four realistic runners left to take the last seat for the Championship with Wolves and Burnley.

We make West Ham 7-4 favourites to fill it, with Forest now 9-4 and Leeds 5-1.

And that second-half display by Spurs at Liverpool showed signs of improvement, as did the performance in beating Atletico Madrid in midweek.



Tomorrow’s demotion derby at home to Forest falls into the category of ‘must not lose’ if they are to have any chance of staying up,

We don’t think they will. But since Spurs haven’t kept a clean sheet in any of their last seven games, Tottenham to win and both teams to score looks a runner – and one we’ve boosted to 7-2.

With the Premier League title surely destined for North London, Arsenal are 1-12 to win it, the Quad is now into 14-1.

However, Arsenal would’ve surely preferred an easier opponent than Man City, as they bid to land the first leg in tomorrow’s Carabao Cup final.

The Gunners are 8-11 to lift the cup and 11-8 to win in 90 minutes. As the betting suggests this could be a cagey affair with potentially a single goal deciding it either way.



So we’ve boosted Arsenal to ‘win to nil’ to 7-2, with a nice special on in-form Eberechi Eze to score from outside the box at 10-1.

After the exodus of the English teams in midweek from the Champions League – just Arsenal 9-4 and Liverpool 9-1 remain – we now go 6-4 there’s an English winner.

We tipped up Atletico before the Spurs tie, and they’re into 20-1, but first must get through the boys from Barca!

Finally, this week’s Nap Acca is Arsenal, Fulham, Newcastle, Hull and Southampton all to win at 12-1.

Plus a reminder that our “2-Up” markets on all major leagues means if your team goes two goals clear but doesn’t hold on you still win!



Can’t say fairer than that!

THE REPUTATION of the best league in the world took a bit of a hit this week.

Man City, Chelsea, Newcastle and Spurs all tumbled out of the Champions League at the last-16 hurdle leaving just Arsenal and Liverpool standing.

The Gunners have the best chance of restoring the Prem’s good name, as best-priced 5-2 favourites to win it with Betway.

And they look to be plenty strong enough to dispense with Portuguese champs Sporting Lisbon in the quarters. Then it gets interesting, with the winners of Barcelona-Atletico Madrid lying in wait.



On the other side of the draw, you’d have to fear for unconvincing Liverpool who face a Paris Saint-Germain side that shredded Chelsea.

Then a tie for the ages, with Real Madrid and Bayern Munich, with the Germans heavy favourites to prevail. I’d have to agree to differ on that

Final four prediction: Arsenal v Barcelona, Real Madrid v PSG. (bold)

Final: Arsenal v Real Madrid.

Winners: Arsenal.



CHAMPIONS LEAGUE OUTRIGHTS (best price): 5-2 Arsenal; 7-2 Bayern; 5-1 Barca; 6-1 PSG; 8-1 Real Madrid; 9-1 Liverpool; 20-1 Atletico; 80-1 Sporting.

Dark Horse

20-1: IF you fall on the City side of the fence, you have their trophy-winning prowess in your favour – yet they’re still odds-against to lift the trophy. Put Kai Havertz and Omar Marmoush (both with Impact Sub) both to score anytime, and City to win the tie and get 20-1 from William Hill.

TV Previews

Brighton v Liverpool

LIVERPOOL’S push to secure a top-four spot and Champions League football next term faces a serious test at Brighton this lunchtime.

The Reds have lost twice and drawn once on their last three visits – and were arguably a better side then.



And European football is back on Albion’s agenda after winning three of their last four.

Shock home win and Dominik Szoboszlai to score anytime at 4-1 with BetVictor are the selections.

FORECAST: 2-1

Newcastle v Sunderland

“FIRST half good, second half not so good,” as Sven-Goran Eriksson might say.



With 45 minutes gone on Wednesday, Newcastle were holding Barcelona 2-2. It finished 7-2.

Only one thing could be worse than that – losing tomorrow’s derby at home to Sunderland.

They won’t. Toon have just beaten Man United and Chelsea, while the Black Cats just lost at Port Vale.

Home win and Joelinton to score anytime at 9-2 with BetVictor.

FORECAST: 3-0



Tottenham v Forest

THIS time last week, I’d have said Tottenham were gone, relegated.

Not now after two brilliant performances out of nowhere to take a point at Anfield and then, forlornly, to beat Atletico Madrid in the Champions League.

Forest, meanwhile, looked a side in trouble as they clung to a point at home to Fulham last weekend.

Spurs to win tomorrow’s titanic relegation tussle and Xavi Simons to score anytime at 19-5 with Hills.



FORECAST: 3-1

Everton vs Chelsea preview

Wounded Chelsea head to Merseyside for Saturday’s Premier League clash with Everton.

It’s been a tough week for Liam Rosenior’s side, who crashed out of the Champions League 8-2 on aggregate after two convincing defeats to French heavyweights Paris Saint-Germain.

They also suffered a tame 1-0 home loss to Newcastle between those heavy defeats, meaning the Londoners have now lost three on the spin.

Chelsea have slipped to sixth in the Premier League, three points off fourth-placed Aston Villa, and face a real battle to avoid Thursday night football next season – with the FA Cup now their only shot at silverware.

But turning things around won’t be straightforward. Chelsea have won just one of their last eight visits to Everton and have struggled defensively on the road, keeping just one clean sheet in their last 15 away games.

That said, after a 2-0 win in December, Chelsea are chasing a first league double over Everton since 2016-17 – while the Toffees have failed to score in 12 of their last 19 Premier League meetings with the Blues.

Speaking of the hosts, Everton are aiming to win back-to-back Premier League games at the Hill Dickinson Stadium for the first time following their 2-0 victory over Burnley.



David Moyes’ side impressed despite a 2-0 defeat at Arsenal last weekend and could move to within two points of Chelsea in sixth with a win on Saturday.

We fancy the hosts to capitalise on tired Chelsea legs after a hectic March schedule – six games compared to Everton’s two – with the visitors also managing just one clean sheet in their last 15 away matches.

SunSport betting experts have previewed the contest and selected the best bets, tips and exclusive sign-up bonuses from our leading betting partners.

Brighton vs Liverpool preview

An unconvincing period for Liverpool was emphatically ended in midweek with a more-than-convincing demolition of Galatasaray.

Arne Slot’s side were utterly dominant in overturning their first leg deficit to dump the Turkish giants out and reach the last-eight of the Champions League.



Back in that competition is where they want to be next season but face a real fight for that spot given their league form.

Their last two league outings saw them record dismal draw at home to Spurs, which followed a defeat to toilers Wolves.

They go into the weekend in fifth place, two points off Aston Villa in fourth and a point clear of Chelsea in sixth.

Brighton will still have their eyes on European football too, sitting in 12th spot but on 40 points, with just five points to Brentford in seventh.



The Seagulls were thumped by Liverpool at Anfield in the FA Cup in March but have won three of their four games since, with their only defeat coming when dominant but losing 1-0 to Arsenal.

They’ve picked up 24 points from a possible 45 at home, with Liverpool taking 21 points on their travels from the same 45 available.

Football.title: Premier League star stuns fans as he plays a Peaky Blinder in Netflix promo as they say ‘man of many talents’
Football.content:
A PREMIER LEAGUE star has stunned fans after stepping into the world of Peaky Blinders for a Netflix promo.

The footballer ditched his kit for a sharp suit and flat cap in a dramatic crossover clip.





The video, released online, quickly went viral as supporters spotted the unexpected cameo.

Styled like a Birmingham gangster, the ace looked right at home in the gritty setting.

Cash, 28, even delivered a brooding line in character, echoing the tone of the hit series.

The Aston Villa full-back is enjoying a standout season, with the club currently sitting fourth in the Premier League.

They are looking to close the four-point gap on Manchester United just above them.

The promo was designed to build hype for the upcoming Peaky Blinders film.

Netflix leaned into football’s global appeal — and fans lapped it up.

Social media lit up with reactions as viewers praised the surprising appearance.



BEST ONLINE CASINOS – TOP SITES IN THE UK

Although some fans were left confused after watching the clip.

One fan wrote: “I wonder what Villa fans think of this”

Another added: “This is gold.”

A third joked: “Sign him up for acting full-time.”



The clip also featured a clever football twist, blending drama with match-day tension.

There was even a subtle nod to VAR, adding a humorous edge to the scene.

Fans were quick to point out how natural the Premier League star looked on camera.

Some even said he “owned the role” despite it being a one-off promo.

Football.title: Premier League goalie at centre of massive SIX-club tug-of-war after dramatic title-charging heroics
Football.content:
BRIGHTON goalkeeper Carl Rushworth is attracting immense Premier League interest amid his stunning loan stint at Coventry, reports suggest.

Rushworth, 24, has played a key role in the Sky Blues’ impressive campaign that has them topping the second flight and in the driving seat for promotion.





The keeper has been a revelation in the second tier keeping a Championship-high 13 clean sheets and only conceding 40 goals across his 38 league appearances.

According to talkSPORT, the ex-England Under-21 international’s heroics at Coventry Building Society Arena have attracted interest from SIX Premier League clubs ahead of next season.

This poses as a major blow for Coventry boss Frank Lampard, who wants to hold onto Rushworth and sign him one a permanent deal amid his team’s push for the Championship title.

Lampard rates his No1 so highly that he even tipped him for a future England call-up.

Brighton are believed to value the shot-stopper in the region of £20million.

And the Seagulls could opt to keep the ex-England youth international after his impressive season in the Championship.

But Fabian Hurzeler’s side may have to move fast as the goalkeeper currently only has one year remaining on his contract at the Amex Stadium.

Rushworth has been with Brighton since 2019 but has never played with the first team.



BEST ONLINE CASINOS – TOP SITES IN THE UK

The goalie has spent almost the entirety of his seven-year spell on the Seagulls’ books out on loan.

Rushworth has played for the likes of Worthing, Walsall, Lincoln, Swansea and Hull.

But it is with Coventry where he got his big break as he has played every minute of their Championship campaign.

Football.title: Bournemouth boss strongly disagrees with Michael Carrick on Man Utd controversy
Football.content:
Bournemouth boss strongly disagrees with Michael Carrick on Man Utd controversy

Manchester United were held to a 2-2 draw by Bournemouth on Friday night but the Red Devils were left furious after seeing a penalty turned down by referee Stuart Attwell

View 2 Images Andoni Iraola has responded to Man United's penalty appeals (Image: Getty Images)

Bournemouth boss Andoni Iraola quickly shut down Manchester United’s appeals for a second penalty in the two teams' controversial 2-2 draw.

The Red Devils were held to a draw by Iraola’s side on the South Coast on Friday evening.

Captain Bruno Fernandes had given United the lead from the penalty spot for a foul on Matheus Cunha. The visitors thought they were deserving of a second penalty, having seen Amad taken down in the area.

‌



But referee Stuart Attwell was unmoved, while VAR also decided not to intervene. United’s fury grew as Bournemouth equalised just seconds later through Ryan Christie.

Asked for his take on the incident, Bournemouth head coach Iraola believed the original decision was correct.

FOLLOW OUR MAN UTD FB PAGE! Latest United news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

Furious Bruno Fernandes calls out VAR as Man Utd question two penalty decisions vs Bournemouth READ MORE :

Man Utd told why VAR allowed controversial Bournemouth goal after Amad penalty decision READ MORE :

Speaking to BBC's Match of the Day, he said: "Now we have the advantage we can see replays quickly and I didn't think there was anything in that one [Amad]. I think the Evanilson one and the Matheus Cunha one are both penalties because defenders lose the position and then the Amad one is never a penalty.

"We asked for one in the first half - Harry Maguire with two hands behind his back but I don't think that was enough for a penalty either. I don't think there is much to discuss."

One man who would certainly disagree with his take is United boss Michael Carrick, who hit out after the full-time whistle. He said: “For him [Stuart Atwell] to give one penalty and not the other… I find it crazy. I cannot understand it. Mental. It’s astonishing. One of them must be wrong.

‌



“A big opportunity to go 2-0 up and we get one penalty and not the other. It's exactly the same thing really - a two-handed grab. He's [referee] got one wrong so, I don't know which one but he doesn't give us the second one.

View 2 Images Amad thought he should have had a penalty against Bournemouth (Image: Getty Images)

“I think they're both penalties and it's a massive moment in the game and it ended up chaos after that. Massive moment and I don't understand how you can give one and not the other - it's crazy.

‌



"It's as obvious as you can get - you've already given one so to not give the other one. It's clear, if that's what he believes is a penalty to start with then the second one has to be. I don't understand how you can't give that.

“And then the goal and after that it was chaos. I was really pleased when we went to 10 men we saw the game out and took a point that's a real positive.

“In the end the way it went you can lose those games so to take a point int he end we'll take that but disappointed not to take all three.”

Football.title: 'I played for Newcastle but I'm all for Sunderland's visitors jab and ruthless team photo'
Football.content:
EXCLUSIVE: 'I played for Newcastle but I'm all for Sunderland's visitors jab and ruthless team photo'

Sunderland AFC achieved a 1-0 win over Newcastle United in December's Wear-Tyne Derby, with the Black Cats poking fun at their most fierce rivals in the aftermath

View 2 Images Sunderland had their revenge on Newcastle with a team picture in December (Image: Sunderland AFC via Getty Images)

Danny Simpson saw no problem with Sunderland AFC's decision to mock Newcastle United with an iconic team photo in the aftermath of December's Wear-Tyne Derby. The Magpies suffered a 1-0 loss to the Black Cats at the Stadium of Light earlier this season, with Nick Woltemade's own-goal making the difference in a tightly contested match.

‌



Newcastle skipper Bruno Guimaraes labelled his team's performance as "embarrassing" in the aftermath, while claiming that they have a better side. However, it was a different gesture at full-time on Sunderland's part at which dominated the headlines.

‌



Having refused to display Newcastle's name on the scoreboard for the duration of the encounter, simply referring to their bitter neighbours as 'visitors', Sunderland took the opportunity to exact their revenge by posing for a team photograph in front of the fans following the win.

‌



OPINION Jeremy Cross Newcastle are treading water under Eddie Howe and their owners need to face reality

The picture, which has now been immortalised by Topps Now in the form of a trading card, was a response on Sunderland's part to a similar photograph by United following a 3-0 FA Cup loss at the Stadium of Light in January 2024.

While the pettiness of the 'visitors' gesture and the photograph will have ultimately rattled Newcastle fans on Tyneside, a former full-back who represented both clubs - Simpson - took no issue. Speaking exclusively to Mirror Football via 7bet online casino, he explained: "Yeah, listen, why not? I'm all here for that.

‌



"The rivalry is right up there with one of the best rivalries in the country. I'm here for a bit of light-hearted banter as well. These families, the boyfriends and girlfriends are on the other side of the table and I'm sure they all do it in their own time as well.

"So yeah, why not? I think it just adds to it. If it was me, it'd wind me up a little bit more to what I get stuck in."

JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! Latest news, analysis and much more on Mirror Football's Facebook page

‌



Elsewhere, the 39-year-old who played for Sunderland on loan in 2007 and enjoyed a three-year permanent stint with Newcastle, added: "I just hope the ref understands the game and lets a few go. You know what I mean?

"Sometimes you've got to manage the occasion. You don't want to ruin that game by sending people off. I remember I had an incident with [Danny] Welbeck in a 50-50 and he’s my friend and I smashed him.

"And I knew I had to, do you know what I mean? Because the fans are there with you. So I just hope the ref understands that and lets a few go because it's what it means to everybody. It's a bit of passion and fight."

‌



On what makes the derby so special for football fans worldwide, Simpson added: "Yeah, I've played in lots of big games, but like I said, I think the build-up, the week, when you go to the petrol station, the shop, the restaurant, it's all everyone speaks about.

View 2 Images Newcastle took a team photograph on the pitch at the Stadium of Light in January (Image: Newcastle United via Getty Images)

"It reminds you of building up for a cup final, if that's the best way I can describe it. You can't read the atmosphere. Obviously, I've played at both stadiums, but it was just incredible.

‌



"It's such a good feeling to come out as a winner of games. I hope, obviously, Newcastle fans, as the players, get to experience that now, especially at St James. I think it's been so long."

He continued: "Obviously, with the league, you want to finish above Sunderland, like Newcastle fans do. It's a massive game, because there's only two points splitting the difference now between them. I think this will be extra special, and I'm glad I'm going to be there to be watching."

Newcastle United play host to Sunderland AFC at St James' Park on Sunday, March 22. Kick-off between the two teams is slated for 12pm.

Football.title: Max Dowman knows what JJ Gabriel is going through with Man Utd star's Premier League ban
Football.content:
Max Dowman knows what JJ Gabriel is going through with Man Utd star's Premier League ban

Manchester United's JJ Gabriel cannot play in the Premier League this season due to age restrictions

View 3 Images JJ Gabriel is waiting in the wings for his opportunity (Image: WM Sport Media - MUFC/Manchester United via Getty Images)

JJ Gabriel might be one of England's most promising talents at present, but he is currently ineligible to play for Manchester United due to his age. To compete in England's Premier League, players must have reached 15 years of age before August 31, meaning Gabriel has yet to progress to the United first team.

‌



This mirrors the situation of Arsenal's Max Dowman, who has recently made his debut for the Gunners. Dowman made history by scoring against Everton last weekend and becoming the youngest player ever to score in the Premier League.

‌



The 16-year-old surpassed Cesc Fabregas' record for Arsenal and is flourishing in north London. However, there was a period when he couldn't feature for the Gunners' first team, due to a Premier League rule prohibiting those under the age of 16.

‌



Dowman didn't satisfy the strict age criteria in 2024, so the teenager had to wait until August 2025 to make his Premier League debut for Arsenal. He came on against Leeds United earlier this season and earned a penalty in the same match.

JJ Gabriel has already made good on Marcus Rashford prediction as Man Utd update arrives READ MORE :

Now, Gabriel will have to adhere to these same regulations and bide his time to perform for United at the highest level. He has already been training with the first team and supporters are eagerly anticipating his debut.

FOLLOW OUR MAN UTD FB PAGE! Latest United news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

‌



However, the fact that the youngster only celebrated his 15th birthday last October has prevented him from being included in a matchday squad. There exists an incredibly narrow timeframe for Gabriel to shatter a record that has remained unbroken for nearly 70 years.

As the 2026/27 season kicks off, the youngster will have approximately eight weeks to make his debut and eclipse former United goalkeeper David Gaskell's record. The goalie was a mere 16 years and 19 days old when he made his official debut in the 1956 FA Charity Shield - now referred to as the Community Shield.

3 View 3 Images Max Dowman is already lighting up the Premier League (Image: Visionhaus/Getty Images) 3 View 3 Images Gabriel is ready to follow in Dowman's footsteps (Image: Zohaib Alam - MUFC/Manchester United via Getty Images)

‌



This implies that Gabriel must maintain his scorching form, avoid injuries and sufficiently impress the new United manager in the summer to earn a call-up. Gabriel boasts 15 goals in 20 appearances for the under-18s across all competitions this season and has secured three caps for England's under-16s.

Current interim boss Michael Carrick has expressed admiration for the teenager as he confirmed Gabriel's exclusion from Premier League action ahead of their journey to Bournemouth. He said: "He's not allowed, he's too young.

"He's doing really well, JJ. We've got some really good young players in the academy and we try and get the younger players up [with the first-team] as much as we can.

Article continues below

"We're always trying to give that exposure to the players to come and train and feel it."

Football.title: Mohamed Salah's value to Liverpool made clear by Arne Slot U-turn before Brighton game
Football.content:
Mohamed Salah's value to Liverpool made clear by Arne Slot U-turn before Brighton game

Mohamed Salah will miss Liverpool's Premier League clash with Brighton on Saturday due to injury but his importance to Arne Slot's side is beyond doubt

View 2 Images Mohamed Salah has shown his value to Liverpool countless times (Image: DARREN STAPLES, AFP via Getty Images)

Liverpool take on Brighton in the Premier League on Saturday and the absence of the injured Mohamed Salah will be keenly felt. His absence - and the reaction to it - shows just how much has changed since these two sides last met in the reverse fixture at Anfield.

‌



Four months ago, Salah was relegated to the bench for three straight games, staying as an unused sub against West Ham and Leeds and only playing 45 minutes against Sunderland. He was omitted altogether for the Champions League league phase clash with Inter Milan, raising questions over his future.

‌



Concerns mounted that last season's leading goalscorer might never regain his form and that his time on Merseyside had come to an end. He made his comeback against the Seagulls, though, with Slot showing a willingness to welcome the player back after that spell out of the starting XI.

‌



The scheduling of the Africa Cup of Nations may have proved beneficial, with Salah travelling to Morocco following that Brighton victory and enjoying a period away from the club spotlight. Nevertheless, his remarks about his relationship with Slot left some worried he could depart during the winter transfer window.

FOLLOW OUR LIVERPOOL FB PAGE! Latest Reds news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

Premier League's Champions League qualification explained if Aston Villa win the Europa League READ MORE :

"I could not believe I was sitting on the bench for 90 minutes," Salah said after the 3-3 draw with Leeds. "That's the third time. It's the first time in my career I think. I am very disappointed, I have done so much for this club over years, especially last season, It seems like the club has thrown me under the bus.

‌



"That's how I feel it. Someone wants me to get all the blame. The club promised me a lot in summer. Now I'm on bench so I can say they haven't kept those promises.

"I used to have a good relationship (with Slot). Now we don't have any relationship and I don't know why. Seems like someone does not want me in the club. I called my mum and dad and told them to come to the Brighton game."

View 2 Images (Image: Robbie Jay Barratt - AMA, Getty Images)

‌



However, Salah returned and featured in seven consecutive league matches before finding himself back amongst the substitutes against Spurs in their most recent outing. He has contributed one goal and three assists in the league since his return from AFCON, alongside four goals and two assists across six appearances in the Champions League and FA Cup.

The most recent of those came in the Champions League this week against Galatasaray. Salah netted his 10th goal of the campaign to help complete a turnaround and it served as yet another reminder of what the Egyptian brings to the table and how dramatically his circumstances have shifted.

Content cannot be displayed without consent

‌



Slot's readiness to reintegrate Salah into the squad in December demonstrates just how vital he and Liverpool consider one of last season's title-winning heroes. Now the challenge is determining how effectively they can manage when he isn't available.

Salah will miss Saturday's fixture at the Amex Stadium. However, he is anticipated to return to action following the international break.

Football.title: Liverpool news: Reds bosses forced into U-turn as Trent Alexander-Arnold suffers blow
Football.content:
Liverpool news: Reds bosses forced into U-turn as Trent Alexander-Arnold suffers blow

Liverpool's owners have performed a dramatic U-turn while there's bad news for former Red Trent Alexander-Arnold

View 3 Images Trent Alexander-Arnold had hoped to make an impact on England manager Thomas Tuchel (Image: TNT Sports)

Liverpool will face Brighton on Saturday afternoon with the mood around the club transformed. Arne Slot's side hit Galatasaray for four on Wednesday evening in the second leg of their Champions League last-16 encounter to reach the quarter-finals.

‌



A dominant display at Anfield looks to have pushed recent struggles to the back of fans' minds. However, the Reds know they cannot slip up against the Seagulls with qualification for next year's Champions League hanging in the balance.

‌



FOLLOW OUR LIVERPOOL FB PAGE! Latest Reds news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

‌



Today's Liverpool news sees club stewards Fenway Sports Group (FSG) grapple with a major U-turn, while former Reds star Trent Alexander-Arnold has been dealt a serious blow. Mirror Football takes a closer look.

FSG U-turn

Liverpool owners FSG have abandoned plans to launch a multi-club model despite talks having taken place. FSG confirmed plans to purchase a second football club in 2024.

The return of Liverpool's former sporting director, Michael Edwards, who left in 2022 and re-joined in 2024, as chief executive of football, was also thought to be propelled by FSG's desire to secure another club.

‌



Mohamed Salah injury update as Arne Slot reveals latest on Liverpool star READ MORE :

Early discussions were held, with as many as 25 clubs being put forward. French side Bordeaux were among the possibilities, while Spanish duo Malaga and Getafe along with Monaco were also considered.

According to The Athletic, plans have been shelved, with no immediate indication that they will be revived any time soon. President Mike Gordon had told staff back in 2024 that the approach had been launched.

View 3 Images Decisions at Liverpool are made by John W Henry and Tom Werner (Image: Getty Images)

‌



In an email to staff, he wrote: "To remain competitive, we must identify every avenue available to us to gain an edge." FSG have owned Liverpool since 2010 and have overseen arguably the club’s most successful period in their modern history.

The Reds have won two Premier League titles, the Champions League, the FA Cup, the League Cup and more since 2019. While plans over multi-club ownership are not progressing, FSG boasts a number of other sports teams in their portfolio, including MLB side Boston Red Sox.

Trent Alexander-Arnold blow

Trent Alexander-Arnold has been omitted from Thomas Tuchel’s last England squad before this summer’s World Cup. Despite an upturn in form at Real Madrid in recent weeks, the former Liverpool man did not make the Germans' expanded 35-man group for the friendlies against Uruguay and Japan.

‌



The defender has not played for England since a 1-0 win over Andorra in June, but speaking after Real’s Champions League win on Tuesday, he revealed he hadn’t given up hope of playing at this summer’s World Cup.

"I've not spoke to him [Tuchel], but I know the squad gets announced this week, so hopefully I get the call and I can play for my country, which is always the greatest honour," he told Amazon Prime.

View 3 Images Trent Alexander-Arnold has endured a mixed season (Image: Getty Images)

‌



The right-back called time on his Liverpool career last year, having been at the Anfield club since the age of six. He departed the Reds having won two Premier League titles in five years, the Champions League, the Club World Cup, the FA Cup, two League Cups and the UEFA Super Cup.

However, life at the Santiago Bernabeu did not start as planned, as multiple injuries reduced his playing time. Xabi Alonso was also sacked as manager adding to the discord, but he has featured in his side’s last nine games in all competitions.

Football.title: Man Utd news: Wayne Rooney snubs Bruno Fernandes as midfielder told he should leave the club
Football.content:
Man Utd news: Wayne Rooney snubs Bruno Fernandes as midfielder told he should leave the club

Michael Carrick has impressed so far as Manchester United boss and Bruno Fernandes has continued to perform well under him

View 3 Images Wayne Rooney has spoken about Manchester United captain Bruno Fernandes (Image: The Overlap)

Michael Carrick has hit the ground running in his second spell as Manchester United's interim manager. Bruno Fernandes is one star in particular who has benefitted from his regime.

‌



Ruben Amorim was given the boot at United in January after a dismal run of form, when he particularly caught criticism for his team selection. Club captain Fernandes was often identified as being played slightly out of position.

‌



Under Carrick, the Portuguese star has thrived but club legend Wayne Rooney still chose to snub the 31-year-old as Mirror Football brings you all the latest from Old Trafford.

‌



FOLLOW OUR MAN UTD FB PAGE! Latest United news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

Wayne Rooney's snub

Appearing on Stick to Football, Rooney and fellow icons Roy Keane and Gary Neville were asked whether or not Fernandes has earned a place in an all-time United XI.

Man Utd issue statement after former coach Jose Mourinho hired dies aged 67 READ MORE :

Michael Carrick gives worryingly honest injury update on Man Utd star READ MORE :

"I think Bruno is a top player but to get into Manchester United's all-time XI, I don't think so," said Rooney. "Well, you think you've got [Eric] Cantona, you've got all the midfielders and all the attacking players. I just don't think, just on stats alone."

‌



View 3 Images Rooney snubbed Fernandes (Image: Getty)

Neville chimed in: "You are talking about getting in front of people like Bobby Charlton in that attacking midfield position. [George] Best, Charlton, [Paul] Scholes…"

Keane also had a say: "What if Bruno leaves in the next couple of years and he's not got even a couple of league titles under his belt? Part of your greatness has to be getting your hands on trophies, it has to be."

‌



Manuel Ugarte told to leave

Midfielder Manuel Ugarte arrived at Old Trafford in the summer of 2024 from Paris Saint-Germain and although he promised to be a key midfield cog, he has disappointed.

The move could cost United up to £50million if specific add-ons are met and he penned a five-year deal that expires in 2029. But fellow countryman and former Premier League ace Gus Poyet has seen enough of the 24-year-old in England.

Asked if Ugarte should seek an exit this summer, Poyet told GOAL: Yeah, and it has affected me a lot because he's from Uruguay and before he went to Paris Saint-Germain, I wanted him to come to Chelsea.

‌



View 3 Images Ugarte has been urged to seek a transfer (Image: Getty)

"I don't know him, so I couldn't call him! And he went to Paris Saint-Germain and I was a little bit upset because I thought it was perfect at that time for Chelsea. But after that, he kind of didn't kick on. Paris Saint-Germain and now Man United.

"He needs to find the right club. I think he's a top player. I think he's a top central midfielder. I think he can cover the pitch well. He can cover spaces. He can defend well. But he needs to find the team. He needs to find, I would say, the identity of a club to be able to play every week because he has even lost his main position at the national team."

Football.title: Premier League relegation battle decided by reverse fixtures with just one point in it
Football.content:
Premier League relegation battle decided by reverse fixtures with just one point in it

The Premier League relegation battle is heating up with Leeds United, Tottenham, West Ham and Nottingham Forest scrapping for survival

View 3 Images Tottenham are one of a handful of relegation-threatened top-flight teams (Image: Getty)

Tottenham are one of many sides facing the very real threat of relegation with eight Premier League fixtures left to play. However, a repeat of the reverse results of these remaining matches would see them survive as they battle with Leeds United, West Ham and Nottingham Forest.

‌



Spurs have suffered a woeful campaign which started under Thomas Frank, with Igor Tudor brought in to tackle the crisis in February. Yet he has been unable to fully stabilise matters, though a 1-1 draw against Liverpool last time out in the league ended a six-match losing run.

‌



They face Forest on Sunday afternoon in a crucial relegation six-pointer as West Ham and Leeds also linger dangerously near the drop. When the two sides last met, it was Forest who emerged as comfortable 3-0 winners.

‌



If history were to repeat itself, then Tottenham would sink even deeper into trouble, but what would the final league standings look like if each remaining fixture replicated its reverse? Mirror Football examines the scenario.

JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! Latest news, analysis and much more on Mirror Football's Facebook page

If we assume that both bottom-placed Wolves and second-bottom Burnley are beyond saving this season, then it appears probable that four clubs are presently fighting to avoid occupying the final relegation place.

‌



Kyle Walker lifts lid on retirement plan and names one club he still wants to play for READ MORE :

Igor Tudor in tense Sky Sports interview after Liverpool draw as Spurs boss hits out at question READ MORE :

Leeds, Tottenham, Forest and West Ham all appear in danger of relegation, but if we examine the reverse fixtures and assume their results are replicated, Spurs would survive. Whilst they suffered a 3-0 defeat to Forest last time round, their following opponents, Sunderland, held them to a 1-1 draw in January.

They then face Brighton and in this scenario would collect another point, having drawn 2-2 in September last year. Next comes Wolves, who once again shared the spoils with Tottenham last time in a 1-1 draw. Aston Villa follow and matters continue to appear fairly grim in our projection as they lost 2-1 to Unai Emery's side in October.

View 3 Images Forest face Spurs on Sunday (Image: Getty)

‌



Nevertheless, things then improve. Spurs defeated Leeds 2-1 when these two teams last met and so crucial three points could be secured here.

Chelsea are up next and this resulted in a 1-0 defeat in the reverse fixture, but they conclude the season against Everton, whom they thrashed 3-0 back in October.

This would see Tottenham finish the campaign on 39 points and if we consider the other relegation-threatened teams' reverse fixtures, they'd avoid the theoretical drop.

‌



View 3 Images West Ham are in danger of going down (Image: Getty)

In this exercise of speculation, Leeds would secure victories against Wolves and West Ham and claim points from draws versus Brentford, Manchester United and Bournemouth. This would leave them safe from relegation too on 41 points.

Forest wouldn't enjoy the same fortune, securing victory against Spurs but managing only draws with Burnley and Manchester United, leaving them on 34 points. However, they would still avoid the drop in this scenario, with West Ham suffering relegation to the Championship.

Article continues below

The Hammers also collect four additional points, courtesy of a victory over Newcastle and a draw with Everton. This would leave them stranded on 33 points and relegated by a single point, although naturally all these clubs can change their fate should they reverse these results in the return fixtures.

Football.title: Arsenal news: Mikel Arteta dealt two huge headaches on eve of Carabao Cup final
Football.content:
Arsenal news: Mikel Arteta dealt two huge headaches on eve of Carabao Cup final

Mikel Arteta’s Arsenal team have the chance to win their first trophy of the season when they head to Wembley on Sunday

View 2 Images Mikel Arteta has plenty to think about ahead of the Carabao Cup final (Image: NEIL HALL/EPA/Shutterstock)

Arsenal can win the first of four potential trophies this season when they take on Manchester City in Sunday’s Carabao Cup final. Victory at Wembley would represent a second cup win for Mikel Arteta since he took over in North London - and a first since the 2020 FA Cup final - but the manager has been given plenty to think about in the days leading up to the game.

‌



Opponents City are also the one team who still have a realistic chance of denying Arsenal the Premier League title. There is a nine-point gap between the two sides but Pep Guardiola's side have eight games left to play while Arsenal have just seven.

‌



The Premier League’s top two could also still meet in the FA Cup, where the quarter-final draw has pitted City against Liverpool and Arsenal against Southampton. They can no longer meet in Europe, though, with Arteta’s men staying alive with a 3-1 aggregate win over Bayer Leverkusen but Guardiola’s side eliminated at the hands of Real Madrid.

‌



Both managers will have spent the last few days doing their preparation for Sunday’s match at Wembley but Arteta has been handed some less-than-welcome news. Here are our latest lines from North London.

FOLLOW OUR ARSENAL FB PAGE! Latest Gunners news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

Arsenal land £60m transfer boost as Champions League advantage clear READ MORE :

Arsenal chief Josh Kroenke travels for Carabao Cup final as transfer plan emerges READ MORE :

Arteta’s keeper headache

Former Arsenal star Paul Merson is torn on Mikel Arteta's goalkeeper conundrum ahead of the Carabao Cup final. Kepa Arrizabalaga has started all five games in the competition so far and Arteta needs to decide whether to stick with his cup keeper or bring in first-choice stopper David Raya.

‌



“Kepa has been first-choice in the Carabao Cup and I think they should stick with him,” Merson wrote for Sportskeeda . “It's only fair because he played well and got you to this stage.

“But I must admit, Raya is world-class! The saves he made against Everton and Leverkusen, absolutely phenomenal.

View 2 Images Arteta will have to choose between David Raya and Kepa Arrizabalaga at Wembley (Image: Getty Images)

‌



“However, if I were the manager, I would still stick to my principles and choose Kepa as the keeper for the final. He's not let the team down in this competition and deserves to start.”

Merson started Arsenal’s last League Cup final victory, against Sheffield Wednesday in 1993. They have lost the final three times since, including against Manchester City in 2018.

City given fixture list boost

Manchester City have been handed a boost as they look to reel in Arsenal in the final few weeks of the season. Mikel Arteta might have hoped to see the Gunners’ rivals stung by fixture congestion around their game in hand but one potential date has been ruled out.

‌



Content cannot be displayed without consent

City will need to find a date to take on Crystal Palace after their meeting was postponed due to the Carabao Cup final. Arsenal’s game against Wolves was brought forward, with that game also initially scheduled for the same weekend as the final, though City couldn’t face Palace at the same time because the Eagles had a Conference League play-off the following day.

Pep Guardiola’s side were eliminated from the Champions League on Tuesday, opening up the possibility of playing their rearranged league game at the same time as Arsenal’s European quarter-final. However, Palace’s progress in Europe - they beat AEK Larnaca after extra-time on Thursday - has put paid to that possibility.

Article continues below

This means, as things stand, City only play four times to Arsenal’s six in April and may well wait until May to face Palace. Arsenal and City will face off in a crucial league game on the third weekend of April and the latter could be significantly better-rested by the time the match kicks off.

The Dan Skelton-trained Getawhisky ran out an impressive winner of a novices' hurdle at Windsor in January before finishing well to claim fourth behind Khrisma at Market Rasen. Fahrenheit Seven relished the return to the minimum distance when scoring at Newcastle 43 days ago and that form gives him an obvious each-way chance under top pilot Tom Marquand.

Brosay makes appeal on his first start for Charlie Pike and Kylian is another to note on debut for Robert Cowell.

Football.title: Im clings on to lead at Valspar Championship as Hovland misses cut
Football.content:
Sungjae Im held onto his lead at the Valspar Championship after rallying on the back nine to edge ahead of David Lipsky.

After missing two straight cuts in his return from a wrist injury, Im shot a two-under 69 to get to nine under par - a stroke ahead of Lipsky in firm and fast conditions on the Copperhead course at Innisbrook.

Im had three bogeys and two birdies in a front-nine 37, then birdied the par-five 11th and par-four 12th. He broke a tie for the lead with a seven-foot birdie putt on the par-three 17th.

The 27-year-old South Korean player has two PGA Tour victories, the 2020 Honda Classic and 2021 Shriners Children's Open.

Lipsky shot 65, playing in the first group of the day off the first tee. Winless on the PGA Tour, he birdied the first two holes and four of the first six, added two more on 10 and 11 and parred the final seven.

Image: Sungjae Im has a one shot lead going into the third round of the Valspar Championship

"It was excellent. I did everything well," Lipsky said. "Missed it in the right spots, holed the putts early on to get some momentum going. That finishing stretch is obviously tough, so able to save a couple pars down on 16 and 18 and really kept the round going."

Chandler Blanchet and Doug Ghim were seven under. Blanchet closed with a bogey for a 66. Ghim shot 67, making an eagle on the par-five 11th, then giving back the strokes with bogeys on 15 and 16.

"Obviously, I wish I would have finished a little bit better," Ghim said. "Two bad holes doesn't really necessarily kind of ruin a good day."

Snedeker had a 72 to drop to five under. The 45-year-old US Presidents Cup captain received a sponsor exemption.

"The good thing is it's playing really tough and tricky this afternoon," Snedeker said. "Try to take a positive from that. Hung in there when things weren't going great."

England's Matt Fitzpatrick, second last week in The Players Championship, was five under with Jordan Smith and Marco Penge.

Brooks Koepka was tied for 10th at four under after a 67 in his return to Innisbrook. He missed a three-foot birdie putt on his final hole.

Defending champion Viktor Hovland missed the cut with rounds of 70 and 75.

Watch round three of The Valspar Championship live on Sky Sports Golf from 11.45am on Saturday. Stream golf contract-free.

Football.title: Devine at the double as Preston beat Stoke
Football.content:
Alfie Devine scored twice as Preston ended a seven-game winless streak with a 3-1 Championship victory over Stoke at Deepdale.

The 21-year-old Tottenham loanee cancelled out a fourth-minute volley from Sorba Thomas with a composed finish in the 15th minute and completed his brace with a delightful dink for Preston's third on the hour mark, just minutes after Milutin Osmajic had headed them in front with his eighth goal of a turbulent season.

Stoke fans were lively from the off - filling three-quarters of the away end - and they were ecstatic when Thomas acrobatically slammed Junior Tchamadeu's cross into the floor and past Daniel Iversen for an early opener.

It was a nightmare start for the out-of-form hosts, however, they bounced back with aplomb 10 minutes later when Stoke over-committed bodies forward.

The visitors left every outfield player in Preston's half as Devine ran onto Andrew Moran's ball from inside his own half before squeezing a shot under the glove of Tommy Simkin to level proceedings.

North End needed that and could have taken the lead in the 23rd minute as Liam Lindsay headed over Devine's corner from an appetising central position.

Visiting supporters were up in arms after 34 minutes as Ben Whiteman clattered Thomas on the halfway line, with Preston looking light in defence, though referee Farai Hallam opted for a yellow card despite calls for a red, given the studs-up nature of the challenge.

Simkin could have done better with the opener and nearly handed Preston a second as he misjudged the flight of Andrew Hughes' cross and the 21-year-old was counting his lucky stars as Osmajic failed to head on target with the goal gaping.

It was one of the best halves of football Preston have had at Deepdale since the turn of the year but Stoke almost equalised early in the second period as Million Manhoef looked set to stab a loose ball past Iversen until Andrija Vukcevic's incredible last-ditch block.

After surviving that scare, Paul Heckingbottom's side soon got a deserved third in the 57th minute with Osmajic making amends for his poor first-half miss by nodding home Lewis Dobbin's pinpoint cross from close-range.

Two quickly became three for the rampant hosts as Osjamic turned provider, slipping Devine in behind a Potters defence that were all at sea and the on-loan Tottenham man delightfully clipped the ball over the advancing Simpkin to bag his eighth goal of the season for the Lilywhites.

Mark Robins' men looked vulnerable to the counter attack throughout and the early second-half substitutes Jesurun Rak-Sakyi, Sam Gallagher and Jun-Ho Bae left them looking even more defensively disjointed, allowing Preston to create that two-goal cushion and see the game out.

The managers

Preston's Paul Heckingbottom:

"Alfie [Devine's] a good player and a good lad. I don't think we've seen the best of him, as good as he's played. There's more to come. He needs pushing but with that as well, you need to understand, he's played a lot of minutes in this league and it's tough.

"You're a young boy, you have peaks and troughs, but where we need to get him is in that consistency of performance because he's got the ability, he's got the brain, and he's got the talent to affect the games on the pitch."

Stoke's Mark Robins:

"It was poor to start with. We just didn't start with the intensity that we needed to play with and played without any quality.

"We've given them chances to get the ball in our box. Not the way we wanted to start the game but they put you under pressure with the way that they play so you have to deal with it.

"We ended up getting our first decent move down the right and Sorba [Thomas] scores. There was an opportunity there to win this game really well tonight and that's the biggest disappointment.

"We've let the supporters down and we know we have."

Football.title: 'Baffling!' Man Utd bemused by penalty calls during draw at Bournemouth
Football.content:
Manchester United boss Michael Carrick said it was "baffling" that Harry Maguire was sent off and gave away a penalty but Bournemouth's Adrien Truffert escaped punishment for an "identical" challenge on Amad Diallo during their 2-2 draw.

Maguire's late foul on Evanilson ultimately proved costly, with Junior Kroupi scoring the resulting spot-kick and United having to hang on with 10 men to take a point from their trip to the south coast.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player FREE TO WATCH: Highlights from Bournemouth’s match against Manchester United in the Premier League

A Bruno Fernandes penalty on the hour mark and James Hill own goal, either side of a fine finish from Ryan Christie, had given United a 2-1 lead before Maguire got his marching orders. But it was an incident just before that Christie goal which left Carrick and United captain Fernandes frustrated.

Amad latched onto a clever pass forward from Fernandes and cut into the box but went to ground when Truffert placed both hands on him. It was similar to Maguire's subsequent foul, which, like Amad, spun Evanilson to the ground.

Monterosa Monterosa , which may be using cookies and other technologies. To show you this content, we need your permission to use cookies. You can use the buttons below to amend your preferences to enable Monterosa cookies or to allow those cookies just once. You can change your settings at any time via the Privacy Options Unfortunately we have been unable to verify if you have consented to Monterosa cookies. To view this content you can use the button below to allow Monterosa cookies for this session only. Enable Cookies Allow Cookies Once

"We should have had another penalty," Carrick told Sky Sports. "Obviously, if you get one, you must get the other.

"It's pretty much identical for me, two-hand grab. Either way, [the ref] got one wrong, but to give one and not give the other, I can't get my head around it. I think it's crazy. It's a bit baffling, really.

"Because of that, they score and then it's chaos after that, really. We should have had another penalty and the game would have been totally different."

Fernandes added: "We get a penalty against [us] where more or less it's the same situation as Amad.

"One is rewarded as a penalty, the other one not. I know it's difficult for the referee to give two penalties in the same game for the same team but what I don't understand is why VAR doesn't get involved in that situation.

"Either one is a penalty and the other one too or none of them is a penalty."

However, Sky Sports pundit Jamie Redknapp took a different view.

"It would have been incredibly harsh," he said. "You can see Truffert is being aggressive with him. He does sort of manhandle him a bit, but I just don't think it's enough for it to be a penalty."

Carrick: We are in a good place

Reflecting on the bigger picture, with his team third in the Premier League and well-placed for Champions League qualification, Carrick said: "We're in a good place. We've put ourselves in this position through some good work, good results and good performances.

"We could have had more tonight but it's not the end of the world.

"We've made some improvements. There are things we can improve on, and we can get a lot better.

"I'm happy with the way things have gone, but we're not satisfied. We definitely want more."

Football.title: St Helens cruise to Toulouse win as Bradford beat Huddersfield
Football.content:
St Helens extended their winning streak to four games as a 30-16 victory at Toulouse lift them up to second place in the Betfred Super League.

The Saints owed their win to a dominant first-half performance in which they scored 28 points in the opening 29 minutes.

Agnatius Paasi opened the floodgates after Owen Dagnall's fourth-minute break from a scrum and Jackson Hastings landed the first of five successful kicks.

Saints built on that fast start as Lewis Murphy crossed twice in the space of eight minutes before the midway point of the first period.

Tristan Sailor set up Murphy's second try and the Australian stand-off produced another superb offload to send David Klemmer over.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Highlights of Wakefield Trinity against Leigh Leopards in Super League

Jake Wingfield then set up Deon Cross and Saints were out of sight before the 30-minute mark.

The start of the second half was an arm wrestle, but Toulouse replied through tries from Benjamin Laguerre, Luke Polselli and Tyler Dupree, with Jake Shorrocks landing two goals.

But the boot of Hastings had the final word as he completed a personal 10-point haul.

Nikorima shines on Bulls debut as Giants slayed

Image: Huddersfield remain winless this season

Bottom-placed Huddersfield suffered a fifth consecutive defeat as Bradford stormed to a 26-14 win.

A tight and tryless first half saw the Bulls lead 4-2 as Luke Hooley landed two goals after Olly Russell's successful sixth-minute kick.

The second period was a far more open affair and Giants regained the lead when Connor Wynne knocked on and Tristan Powell was first to the loose ball, with Russell adding the extras.

Bradford took a 16-8 lead as Leon Ruan and Jayden Nikorima powered over in the space of three minutes and Chris Atkin kicked two conversions.

Huddersfield refused to lie down and Jacob Gagai grounded a bouncing ball after the Bulls failed to deal with a high kick, and Russell's conversion reduced the deficit to two points.

But Bradford finished with a flourish as Nikorima went down the short side three minutes from time and fed Hooley, who dived over in the corner.

Bradford sealed the win and moved up to fifth as Nikorima marked his Bulls debut with a second score, with Hooley adding the final gloss.
